reasoning: The 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship will likely feature a 64-player field, as it did in the past. In a knockout structure, the average player has a 1 in 64 (1.56%) chance of winning. Matt Salsberg is a talented amateur/semi-professional, but he is not widely considered one of the top elite heads-up specialists who would command a significantly higher-than-average win probability in a field packed with top professionals. Factoring in the base rate of winning a 64-player tournament and Salsberg's skill level relative to the likely world-class field, his chance of winning is estimated near the base probability. I assign a slightly elevated 2% probability to account for variance in poker, but limit it at the low bound of the available scale.
